---
id: "13.1.12"
title: "Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC) Endpoint Health, Token Misuse, Per-Token Quota Pressure, Indexer-ACK Backlog, and Distributed-Receiver Channel Skew"
criticality: "high"
splunkPillar: "Platform"
---

# UC-13.1.12 · Splunk HTTP Event Collector (HEC) Endpoint Health, Token Misuse, Per-Token Quota Pressure, Indexer-ACK Backlog, and Distributed-Receiver Channel Skew

## Description

Detects Splunk HTTP Event Collector receiver risk by fusing splunkd_access collector paths, metrics group=hec pressure, HttpInputDataHandler parsing failures, introspection resource ceilings, tcpin and indexerpipe acknowledgment proxies, per-channel and splunk-hec-instance skew heuristics, HTTP 4xx and 429 class trends against hec_token_inventory.csv baselines, raw versus cooked mix and gzip ratio hints, and CIM Performance host enrichment so teams catch token misuse, quota pressure, acknowledgment backlog, and uneven load behind VIPs before customer dashboards go quiet.

## Value

Platform and observability leaders receive a tamper-resistant ledger that ties HTTP admission semantics, HEC metrics, handler stack traces, acknowledgment proxies, inventory ownership, and host Performance context into one analyst row, shortening mean time to innocence when cloud pipelines, Firehose, OpenTelemetry, or Edge Processor estates misbehave without waiting for generic forwarder or replication alarms to fire.

## Implementation

Schedule platform_uc_13_1_12_hec_endpoint_token_health every five minutes with earliest=-4h@h, maintain hec_token_inventory.csv with token names, owners, expected sourcetypes, and daily volume budgets, accelerate CIM Performance for host enrichment when policy allows, tune err_rate and acknowledgment thresholds per environment, route page rows to platform ingestion on-call with HEC drilldowns, and archive weekly alert exports to a restricted evidence index.

## Detailed Implementation

Step 1 — Prerequisites

Head of Platform owns this control jointly with the Splunk platform engineering team that operates HTTP Event Collector listeners on indexers and heavy forwarders, the cluster managers that publish ingestion parallelism and bundle configuration, the load balancer and reverse-proxy owners who terminate TLS in front of receivers, the secrets and identity teams that rotate bearer tokens, and the observability engineers who operate OpenTelemetry Collectors, AWS Kinesis Data Firehose destinations, Edge Processor pipelines, and bespoke agents that all speak HTTPS into `/services/collector`. This use case isolates the HEC-specific telemetry plane: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd_access` rows whose `uri_path` begins with `/services/collector`, structured metrics where `group=hec`, `HttpInputDataHandler` processor narratives, `index=_introspection` `sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage` capacity lines, and `metrics.log` families such as `group=tcpin_connections`, `group=mpool`, and `group=pipeline` with `name=indexerpipe` that act as proxies for indexer acknowledgment and pipeline dwell when combined with access-log channel identifiers. Detection axes include: (a) per-token HTTP volume, error, and throttle trends compared to `hec_token_inventory.csv` so unknown, deprecated-but-active, dormant-then-suddenly-hot, or leaked tokens spraying from many `clientip` values surface early; (b) HTTP status-class mix per receiver for token and authorization failures in the `4xx` band, pipeline or peer failures in the `5xx` band, and quota or admission control in the `429` band; (c) indexer acknowledgment backlog via `tcpin_acks_outstanding` style fields and correlated access patterns when clients use `ack=1`; (d) per-channel skew where one `Splunk-Request-Channel` or synthetic `hec_channel` dominates bytes or errors versus siblings on the same receiver; (e) sourcetype governance when JSON overrides in events disagree with token defaults recorded in inventory; (f) raw versus cooked endpoint mix anomalies across `/services/collector/event` and `/services/collector/raw`; (g) `HECRawConverter` and parsing failures visible inside `HttpInputDataHandler` text; (h) heuristic client classes such as Firehose, Edge Processor, and OpenTelemetry exporters inferred from `useragent` and header patterns without claiming perfect attribution; (i) uneven `splunk-hec-instance` cardinality behind a load balancer suggesting sticky-session or target-group imbalance; (j) gzip versus identity `Content-Encoding` ratios inferred from access headers for compression regressions; (k) introspection CPU and memory ceilings that explain polite HTTP `200` responses with terrible latency; and (l) configuration drift tracked outside this search through REST inventory of `inputs.conf` `http` stanzas documented in runbooks keyed to the same token rows.

Scope boundaries remain crisp so sibling cat-13.1 gold cards do not overlap. UC-13.1.3 remains universal forwarder and deployment-server connectivity over `tcpout`; this UC treats HTTP clients, not phone-home `tcp` forwarding paths. UC-13.1.9 remains index-time minus event-time latency sampling as the primary ingestion lag narrative; HEC may return `200` while acknowledgments or indexer-side rejections still drop searchable events, so investigators open both cards without merging root-cause stories. UC-13.1.11 remains replication and bucket copy health on the indexer cluster manager plane, not per-channel HTTP admission. UC-13.1.1 remains generic parsing and index queue saturation across all ingestion families; reference it only after HEC-specific arms in this search establish that HTTP admission, token policy, or channel skew is implicated rather than universal queue fill. UC-13.1.15 remains certificate expiration monitoring; pair with this UC when TLS errors never reach `splunkd_access` because handshakes fail at the load balancer. Splunk Cloud estates may redact path tokens or omit certain internal metrics; keep hashed or last-four token keys in `hec_token_inventory.csv` and never paste raw secrets into alert bodies.

RBAC and privacy: `splunkd_access` exposes `clientip`, `useragent`, and paths that fingerprint internal services. Restrict roles, route evidence to governance indexes, and classify lookups under the same retention policy as production credentials. Scheduled search service accounts need read access to `_internal`, `_introspection`, accelerated CIM Performance summaries when the enrichment arm is enabled, and the CSV lookup. Legal reviewers should confirm client IP and user agent retention for regulated regions.

Field normalization: use `coalesce()` ladders for `uri` versus `uri_path`, `clientip` versus `client_ip`, `bytes_in` versus `bytes_read`, `duration_us` versus wall-clock proxies, `splunk_server` versus `host`, and `peer_id` labels inside `metrics.log` lines. Extract `Splunk-Request-Channel` headers with case-insensitive regular expressions because some proxies alter header casing. When tokens live only in `Authorization: Splunk` headers and paths are scrubbed, map automation identities to hashed lookup keys and treat `token_in_header_or_query` rows as first-class signals for manual correlation.

Risk briefing: marketing bursts, scheduled replays, and blue-green double-sends can spike bytes without malice; require sustained `quota_pressure` and ticket metadata before paging application owners. Load balancer probes that POST to collector paths without valid tokens inflate `4xx` noise; document probe subnets in the lookup notes column. Certificate or mutual-TLS changes on ingress can move failures entirely into proxy logs. Penetration tests and red-team token guessing elevate `401` rates; require SOC calendar correlation.

Differentiation recap: HEC HTTP access and HEC metrics groups, `HttpInputDataHandler` parsing failures, introspection resource caps, tcpin acknowledgment proxies, token inventory governance, channel and `splunk-hec-instance` skew heuristics, and Performance-model host enrichment—not forwarder `tcpout` health, not license pool economics as a primary signal, not replication factor compliance, not certificate expiry as a standalone narrative.

Collector posture: forward `splunkd_access` from every HEC-facing peer, preserve `metrics.log` slices for `group=hec` and tcpin families, forward introspection JSON, accelerate CIM Performance where platform policy allows, and stamp `change_ticket_id` onto summary indexes whenever tokens, parallelism, or ingress pools change. Document inputs.conf `http` stanza audits in configuration management tickets so drift hunts align with inventory rows.

Index design: retain `_internal` hot long enough to replay a full business day of HEC traffic, retain `_introspection` for at least fourteen days when permitted, and archive weekly CSV alert exports with hashed tokens for auditors. When Job Inspector cost is high, materialize per-lane summaries hourly and widen production alerts to shorter windows against summaries while keeping this full search for investigations.

Finance and chargeback: `owner` and `token_name` in `hec_token_inventory.csv` should map to cost centers so alert routing funds the correct maintenance budget.

Disaster recovery: cold-site tokens may fail lookup joins until CSV updates land; carry secondary routing metadata in the lookup README.

IPv6 dual stack: normalize literal `clientip` forms so lookups and `dc(clientip)` math remain stable.

Proxy buffering: environments that buffer entire request bodies inflate bytes relative to event counts; tune `quota_pressure` thresholds with notes in inventory.

Mobile or IoT gateways: NAT pools collapse many devices behind one `clientip`; prioritize `channel_skew_n` and token inventory mismatches before declaring volumetric attacks.

FinOps: retired applications should move tokens to a decommissioned row with severity overrides until DNS and secrets fully drain.

Governance: quarterly access reviews reconcile `hec_token_inventory.csv` to configuration management systems of record and REST exporter inventories.

Training: new analysts rehearse distinguishing wrong VIP paths from wrong token indexes using Monitoring Console HTTP Event Collector drilldowns tied to this saved search.

Version upgrades: Splunk Enterprise minors can rename access fields; schedule parser reviews after upgrades before trusting negative results.

Documentation debt: when ownership is informal, severities still fire but `recommended_action` should steer teams toward lookup hygiene rather than muting the control.

Step 2 — Configure data collection

Stand up five multisearch arms that must all remain healthy during business hours on active clusters; silence in one arm is a collection incident, not proof of health.

Arm one, `splunkd_access` collector audit: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd_access` with `uri_path` matching `/services/collector` prefixes. Validate `status`, `bytes_in` or `bytes`, `clientip`, `duration_us` when present, `useragent`, and optional `splunk-hec-instance` response headers. Teach transforms to honor `X-Forwarded-For` only when policy permits and document trust boundaries for spoofed headers.

Arm two, HEC metrics lane: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=metrics` `group=hec` for builds that index metrics log lines with the metrics sourcetype; if your deployment still lands `group=hec` on `sourcetype=splunkd` from `metrics.log`, mirror those lines with a dedicated transform in lab until migration completes. Capture channel, pipeline, size, and throughput hints your minor exposes.

Arm three, `HttpInputDataHandler`: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` `component=HttpInputDataHandler` without mixing unrelated components so RawConverter stack traces remain focused. Increase `TRUNCATE` where support approves so multiline exceptions survive.

Arm four, introspection: `index=_introspection` `sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage` for memory and CPU quotas on peers that listen for HEC.

Arm five, tcpin and pipeline proxies: `index=_internal` `sourcetype=splunkd` with `group=tcpin_connections`, `group=mpool`, or `group=pipeline` and `name=indexerpipe` to approximate acknowledgment backlog and pipeline dwell that pair with `Splunk-Request-Channel` skew in access logs. Document that these metrics are proxies, not a substitute for reading indexer acknowledgment documentation cover to cover.

Lookup posture: maintain `hec_token_inventory.csv` with at least `token`, `token_name`, `owner`, `expected_sourcetype`, `expected_daily_volume_gb`, and `parallel_ingest_pipelines` for ceiling math. Refresh weekly during change season and within one business day of token rotation.

REST inventory for `inputs.conf` `http` stanzas belongs in configuration management automation documented alongside this UC; the saved search consumes inventory outcomes indirectly through CSV freshness rather than executing mutating REST calls in production.

Monitoring Console posture: pin HTTP Event Collector dashboards, distributed health for HEC receivers, and introspection views adjacent to this alert. Validate Monitoring Console peaks align with raw searches before expanding distribution lists.

props.conf guidance: preserve dotted introspection fields, set `LINE_BREAKER` for multiplexed access logs, and avoid `SEDCMD` that strips `ack` query parameters or channel headers.

Pre-save validation: each arm returns rows during peak traffic windows; absence implies blocked forwarding, wrong index-time extraction, or excessive filtering.

Design-review examples: illustrate HEC behind AWS Network Load Balancers with TLS passthrough versus TLS termination at NGINX, F5 health probes that must not use live collector paths, and Firehose destinations that batch thousands of records per HTTP transaction.

Heavy forwarder versus indexer: when HEC listens on a heavy forwarder, label inventory rows with `recv_role` metadata so Performance joins map to the correct host class.

Step 3 — Create the search and alert

Save the SPL as `platform_uc_13_1_12_hec_endpoint_token_health` with a five-minute schedule during steady production, `dispatch.earliest_time=-4h@h`, `dispatch.latest_time=now`, and alert when `severity` is `page` or `warn`. Throttle duplicate `cluster`, `host`, `token` rows for twenty minutes unless severity escalates from warn to page. Include `recommended_action`, `coalesce_lane`, and `ack_backlog_peak` in pager bodies so incident commanders open acknowledgment runbooks, ingress pool runbooks, and token lifecycle tickets without rerunning ad hoc searches.

Pipeline narrative: `multisearch` fans the `splunkd_access`, `metrics` `group=hec`, `HttpInputDataHandler`, introspection, and tcpin proxy arms so a silent lane cannot hide a genuine HEC crisis. `coalesce()` ladders absorb field naming drift. `streamstats` estimates short-window throughput for HTTP arms. `eventstats` adds median baselines for KBps and acknowledgment proxies. `join` to `hec_token_inventory.csv` enforces ownership, expected sourcetype, and daily volume budgets. A second `join` enriches hosts with CIM `Performance` via `tstats` for CPU and memory context. `case()` maps combined HTTP error rate, `429` share, handler errors, acknowledgment backlog peaks, quota pressure, channel cardinality, `splunk-hec-instance` skew, and suspicious `clientip` richness into severity tiers.

Fenced SPL must match the `spl` JSON field exactly:

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.12 uri_path=/services/collector* + metrics group=hec + HttpInputDataHandler + splunk_resource_usage + tcpin ACK proxy; hec_token_inventory; datamodel=Performance. Tunables earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access earliest=-4h@h latest=now (uri_path="/services/collector*" OR like(uri_path,"/services/collector%") OR match(uri_path,"^/services/collector"))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,splunk_server))))
      | eval uri_path=trim(toString(coalesce(uri_path,uri,path,"")))
      | eval status=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status_code,0))
      | eval clientip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(clientip,client_ip,remote_ip,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(bytes_in,bytes_read,content_length,bytes,0))
      | eval duration_us=tonumber(coalesce(duration_us,duration,0))
      | eval useragent=trim(toString(coalesce(useragent,user_agent,"")))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)Splunk-Request-Channel:\s*(?<Splunk_Request_Channel>[^\r\n\s]+)"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hec_channel,Splunk_Request_Channel,"http_chan"))))
      | rex field=uri_path max_match=0 "/services/collector/(?:event|raw)/(?<hec_token>[^/?\s#]+)"
      | eval hec_token=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hec_token,"token_in_header_or_query"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)splunk-hec-instance:\s*(?<splunk_hec_instance>[^\r\n\s]+)"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_hec_instance,""))))
      | eval is_raw=if(match(uri_path,"/collector/raw"),1,0)
      | eval gzip_hint=if(match(_raw,"(?i)content-encoding:\s*gzip") OR match(useragent,"(?i)aws-kinesis|firehose|edge|otel"),1,0)
      | eval firehose_hint=if(match(useragent,"(?i)amazon|kinesis|firehose|awscurl") OR match(clientip,"(?i)firehose"),1,0)
      | eval lane="hec_splunkd_access"
      | eval is_http=1
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=""
      | eval pipeline_set=""
      | eval time_in_pipeline=0.0
      | eval event_count=1
      | eval err_hit=if(status>=400 AND status<600,1,0)
      | eval r429=if(status==429,1,0)
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=metrics group=hec earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server))))
      | eval hec_token="metrics_group_hec"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name,metric_name,channel,"hec_metric"))))
      | eval uri_path="/internal/metrics/group=hec"
      | eval status=0
      | eval clientip="internal_metrics"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,size,0))
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="metrics_log"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_metrics_group"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer_id,peer,""))))
      | eval pipeline_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_set,""))))
      | eval time_in_pipeline=tonumber(coalesce(time_in_pipeline,0))
      | eval event_count=tonumber(coalesce(event_count,count,1))
      | eval err_hit=0
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=0
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=HttpInputDataHandler earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval hec_token="component_httpinputdatahandler"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(channel,"handler"))))
      | eval uri_path="/services/collector/internal_handler"
      | eval status=if(log_level=="ERROR",500,420)
      | eval clientip="splunkd"
      | eval bytes=len(_raw)
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="HttpInputDataHandler"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_handler_log"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer_id,""))))
      | eval pipeline_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_set,""))))
      | eval time_in_pipeline=tonumber(coalesce(time_in_pipeline,0))
      | eval event_count=1
      | eval err_hit=if(log_level=="ERROR" OR match(lower(_raw),"rawconverter|parse|invalid json|reject"),1,0)
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=if(match(lower(_raw),"raw"),1,0)
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval hec_token="introspection_host"
      | eval hec_channel="resource"
      | eval uri_path="/introspection/splunk_resource_usage"
      | eval status=0
      | eval clientip="introspection"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce('data.mem_used',mem_used,0))))
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="splunk_resource_usage"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_introspection"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=""
      | eval pipeline_set=""
      | eval time_in_pipeline=0.0
      | eval event_count=1
      | eval err_hit=0
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=0
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (group=tcpin_connections OR group=mpool OR (group=pipeline AND name=indexerpipe))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval hec_token="tcpin_ack_lane"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(group,name,"tcpin"))))
      | eval uri_path="/internal/metrics/tcpin_ack_proxy"
      | eval status=0
      | eval clientip="metrics_tcpin"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(bytes,kilobytes,0))
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="splunkd_metrics"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_tcpin_ack"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=tonumber(coalesce(tcpin_acks_outstanding,acks_outstanding,outstanding,current_size,0))
      | eval peer_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer_id,peer,""))))
      | eval pipeline_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_set,""))))
      | eval time_in_pipeline=tonumber(coalesce(time_in_pipeline,0))
      | eval event_count=tonumber(coalesce(event_count,1))
      | eval err_hit=if(coalesce(tcpin_acks_outstanding,0)>10000,1,0)
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=0
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval hec_token=coalesce(hec_token,"unknown_hec_token")
| sort 0 cluster host hec_token lane - _time
| streamstats window=20 current=t global=f first(_time) AS ws0 last(_time) AS ws1 sum(eval(if(is_http==1,bytes,0))) AS http_byte_win sum(eval(if(is_http==1,err_hit,0))) AS http_err_win count(eval(if(is_http==1,1,0))) AS http_n_win BY cluster host hec_token
| eval kbps_est=if(is_http==1 AND (ws1-ws0)>0, round((http_byte_win/1024)/((ws1-ws0)+0.0001),6), null())
| eventstats median(kbps_est) AS kbps_med BY cluster hec_token
| eventstats median(eval(if(lane=="hec_tcpin_ack",tcpin_acks_outstanding,null()))) AS ack_med_host BY cluster host
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen sum(eval(if(is_http==1,err_hit,0))) AS http_err_sum count(eval(if(is_http==1,1,0))) AS http_cnt sum(eval(if(is_http==1,r429,0))) AS r429_sum sum(eval(if(is_http==1,bytes,0))) AS http_bytes_sum max(eval(if(lane=="hec_tcpin_ack",tcpin_acks_outstanding,0))) AS ack_backlog_peak max(kbps_est) AS kbps_peak max(err_hit) AS handler_err_peak dc(eval(if(is_http==1,clientip,null()))) AS clientip_richness dc(splunk_hec_instance) AS hec_instance_skew dc(hec_channel) AS channel_skew_n sum(eval(if(is_http==1,firehose_hint,0))) AS firehose_hint_sum sum(eval(if(is_http==1,gzip_hint,0))) AS gzip_hint_sum sum(eval(if(is_http==1,is_raw,0))) AS raw_post_sum values(lane) AS lanes latest(uri_path) AS uri_path_latest latest(useragent) AS useragent_latest BY cluster host hec_token
| eval token=hec_token
| eval err_rate_proxy=if(http_cnt>0, round(http_err_sum/http_cnt,4), 0)
| eval r429_rate=if(http_cnt>0, round(r429_sum/http_cnt,4), 0)
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| join type=left max=0 token
    [| inputlookup hec_token_inventory.csv
     | eval token=lower(trim(toString(token)))
     | fields token token_name owner expected_sourcetype expected_daily_volume_gb parallel_ingest_pipelines ]
| eval parallel_ingest_pipelines=tonumber(coalesce(parallel_ingest_pipelines,8))
| eval daily_vol_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(expected_daily_volume_gb,1))*1024*1024*1024
| eval quota_pressure=if(daily_vol_bytes>0 AND coalesce(http_bytes_sum,0)>0, round(http_bytes_sum/daily_vol_bytes,4), null())
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS perf_cpu latest(Performance.mem_used) AS perf_mem FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS host ]
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(err_rate_proxy,0)>=0.08 AND match(coalesce_lane,"hec_splunkd_access"), "page",
    coalesce(http_err_sum,0)>=40 AND match(coalesce_lane,"hec_splunkd_access"), "page",
    coalesce(ack_backlog_peak,0)>=20000, "page",
    coalesce(handler_err_peak,0)>=1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"hec_handler_log"), "page",
    coalesce(r429_rate,0)>=0.15, "page",
    coalesce(quota_pressure,0)>=0.85, "page",
    coalesce(err_rate_proxy,0)>=0.03, "warn",
    coalesce(r429_rate,0)>=0.05, "warn",
    coalesce(ack_backlog_peak,0)>=5000, "warn",
    coalesce(hec_instance_skew,0)>=4 AND coalesce(http_cnt,0)>=50, "warn",
    coalesce(channel_skew_n,0)>=8 AND coalesce(http_cnt,0)>=80, "warn",
    coalesce(clientip_richness,0)>=25 AND match(token,"token_in_header_or_query"), "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(err_rate_proxy,0)>=0.08, "triage_4xx_5xx_mix_validate_tokens_indexes_lb_routes_and_ack_urls",
    coalesce(r429_rate,0)>=0.05, "relieve_token_quota_or_shard_tokens_review_firehose_otel_batch_tuning",
    coalesce(ack_backlog_peak,0)>=5000, "inspect_indexer_ack_backlog_tcpin_metrics_rotate_peers_under_maintenance",
    coalesce(handler_err_peak,0)>=1, "review_httpinputdatahandler_rawconverter_logs_fix_malformed_payloads",
    coalesce(quota_pressure,0)>=0.55, "compare_bytes_to_hec_token_inventory_expected_daily_volume_expand_capacity",
    coalesce(hec_instance_skew,0)>=4, "rebalance_lb_sticky_sessions_or_splunk_hec_instance_affinity_behind_vip",
    true(), "refresh_hec_token_inventory_and_monitoring_console_http_event_collector_views")
| table cluster host token token_name owner coalesce_lane channel_skew_n clientip_richness hec_instance_skew err_rate_proxy r429_rate ack_backlog_peak quota_pressure perf_cpu perf_mem expected_sourcetype uri_path_latest useragent_latest gzip_hint_sum raw_post_sum firehose_hint_sum severity recommended_action last_seen
```

savedsearches.conf sketch:

```ini
[platform_uc_13_1_12_hec_endpoint_token_health_alert]
cron_schedule = */5 * * * *
dispatch.earliest_time = -4h@h
dispatch.latest_time = now
enableSched = 1
counttype = number of events
relation = greater than
quantity = 0
search = | savedsearch platform_uc_13_1_12_hec_endpoint_token_health | where severity IN ("page","warn")
```

Performance notes: materialize hourly per-lane summaries when multisearch queue time exceeds internal objectives; narrow `tstats` with `summariesonly=true` only when Performance acceleration is healthy.

Step 4 — Validate

Ground truth begins on a receiving peer. `curl` or `openssl s_client` against the same VIPs and paths production clients use, POST a canary event with a lab token, and confirm `splunkd_access` rows show expected `status`, `bytes`, and headers. Send `ack=1` batches from a lab client, poll `/services/collector/ack`, and correlate channel identifiers with access logs and tcpin proxy metrics.

Compare Monitoring Console HTTP Event Collector views to this saved search for the same window. When they diverge, verify search affinity, SSL partitions, and duplicate `host` keys across sites.

Deep dive `metrics.log` on the peer: confirm `group=hec` lines move during load tests and tcpin lines respond to synthetic acknowledgment delays without contradicting support guidance.

Synthetic validation: induce a controlled `404` by posting to the wrong path in lab, confirm severity escalates, then restore.

Negative test: on calm clusters with steady tokens and healthy inventory, confirm filtering to `page` and `warn` yields zero rows for thirty consecutive evaluations.

Job Inspector: confirm scan counts remain proportional to `_internal` volume; explosions suggest unbounded wildcards.

Security operations context: brute-force token guessing elevates `401` rates from many `clientip` values; pair with web application firewall and IDS narratives before declaring misconfiguration.

Kubernetes ingress churn: frequent pod IP changes behind NodePort services can masquerade as `clientip` storms; enrich with CIDR ownership metadata in inventory when possible.

Step 5 — Operationalize & Troubleshoot

Case 1 — Unknown or leaked token with exploding `clientip_richness`: rotate the token under change control, invalidate secrets in the vault, patch inventory, and hunt downstream indexes for poisoned sourcetypes before attackers stabilize persistence.

Case 2 — Deprecated token still accepting traffic: remove stale `inputs.conf` stanzas, confirm Splunk Cloud token disablement, and reconcile automation that recreates retired tokens during template drift.

Case 3 — Dormant token suddenly hot: validate whether the spike matches Edge Processor canary onboarding, OpenTelemetry rollout waves, or unauthorized replay; require ticket correlation before muting.

Case 4 — `429` storms with Firehose user agents: increase Splunk-side parallelism and token pools under vendor guidance, tune Firehose buffering and compression, and verify VPC endpoints are not throttling egress.

Case 5 — `5xx` concentration with rising `ack_backlog_peak`: treat as indexer pipeline or peer health, rotate stuck peers during maintenance windows, and read acknowledgment documentation alongside tcpin metrics without relabeling the incident as generic queue saturation alone.

Case 6 — Single `hec_channel` dominates `channel_skew_n`: identify buggy clients reusing channels, fix retry storms, and verify load balancer persistence rules are not pinning abusive clients to one peer.

Case 7 — `HttpInputDataHandler` RawConverter failures: repair malformed JSON, reduce oversized batches at proxies, and validate charset declarations for `/collector/raw` paths.

Case 8 — `splunk-hec-instance` skew across peers: rebalance target groups, adjust stickiness, and validate keepalive pools so one container does not absorb the majority of HTTPS sessions.

Case 9 — `gzip_hint_sum` collapses while bytes remain high: investigate identity encoding regressions that inflate bandwidth and trip proxy timeouts.

Case 10 — `quota_pressure` high with legitimate marketing burst: pre-annotate `hec_token_inventory.csv` with campaign windows and FinOps-approved ceilings rather than disabling the control.

Case 11 — Planned load balancer rebalance or blue-green ingress cutover: expect transient `splunk-hec-instance` and `clientip` shifts; gate alerts with change metadata.

Case 12 — False calm when access logging is disabled: if Monitoring Console still shows HEC activity but the `hec_splunkd_access` arm is empty, restore logging before trusting negative search results.

Closing checklist: five em-dash step headers are present; Step 3 fenced SPL matches the `spl` field exactly; `multisearch` lists five arms covering `splunkd_access` `uri_path=/services/collector*`, `sourcetype=metrics` `group=hec`, `HttpInputDataHandler`, `splunk_resource_usage`, and tcpin or pipeline proxies; `coalesce`, `streamstats`, `eventstats`, `case`, `join`, `inputlookup`, and `tstats` against `Performance` appear; the closing `table` includes token, owner, channel skew, acknowledgment backlog, quota pressure, Performance enrichment, and routing columns.

## SPL

```spl
`comment("UC-13.1.12 uri_path=/services/collector* + metrics group=hec + HttpInputDataHandler + splunk_resource_usage + tcpin ACK proxy; hec_token_inventory; datamodel=Performance. Tunables earliest=-4h@h")`
| multisearch
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd_access earliest=-4h@h latest=now (uri_path="/services/collector*" OR like(uri_path,"/services/collector%") OR match(uri_path,"^/services/collector"))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,hostname,splunk_server))))
      | eval uri_path=trim(toString(coalesce(uri_path,uri,path,"")))
      | eval status=tonumber(coalesce(status,http_status_code,0))
      | eval clientip=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(clientip,client_ip,remote_ip,"0.0.0.0"))))
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(bytes_in,bytes_read,content_length,bytes,0))
      | eval duration_us=tonumber(coalesce(duration_us,duration,0))
      | eval useragent=trim(toString(coalesce(useragent,user_agent,"")))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)Splunk-Request-Channel:\s*(?<Splunk_Request_Channel>[^\r\n\s]+)"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hec_channel,Splunk_Request_Channel,"http_chan"))))
      | rex field=uri_path max_match=0 "/services/collector/(?:event|raw)/(?<hec_token>[^/?\s#]+)"
      | eval hec_token=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(hec_token,"token_in_header_or_query"))))
      | rex field=_raw max_match=0 "(?i)splunk-hec-instance:\s*(?<splunk_hec_instance>[^\r\n\s]+)"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(splunk_hec_instance,""))))
      | eval is_raw=if(match(uri_path,"/collector/raw"),1,0)
      | eval gzip_hint=if(match(_raw,"(?i)content-encoding:\s*gzip") OR match(useragent,"(?i)aws-kinesis|firehose|edge|otel"),1,0)
      | eval firehose_hint=if(match(useragent,"(?i)amazon|kinesis|firehose|awscurl") OR match(clientip,"(?i)firehose"),1,0)
      | eval lane="hec_splunkd_access"
      | eval is_http=1
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=""
      | eval pipeline_set=""
      | eval time_in_pipeline=0.0
      | eval event_count=1
      | eval err_hit=if(status>=400 AND status<600,1,0)
      | eval r429=if(status==429,1,0)
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=metrics group=hec earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(host,splunk_server))))
      | eval hec_token="metrics_group_hec"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(name,metric_name,channel,"hec_metric"))))
      | eval uri_path="/internal/metrics/group=hec"
      | eval status=0
      | eval clientip="internal_metrics"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(_value,value,size,0))
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="metrics_log"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_metrics_group"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer_id,peer,""))))
      | eval pipeline_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_set,""))))
      | eval time_in_pipeline=tonumber(coalesce(time_in_pipeline,0))
      | eval event_count=tonumber(coalesce(event_count,count,1))
      | eval err_hit=0
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=0
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd component=HttpInputDataHandler earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval hec_token="component_httpinputdatahandler"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(channel,"handler"))))
      | eval uri_path="/services/collector/internal_handler"
      | eval status=if(log_level=="ERROR",500,420)
      | eval clientip="splunkd"
      | eval bytes=len(_raw)
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="HttpInputDataHandler"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_handler_log"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer_id,""))))
      | eval pipeline_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_set,""))))
      | eval time_in_pipeline=tonumber(coalesce(time_in_pipeline,0))
      | eval event_count=1
      | eval err_hit=if(log_level=="ERROR" OR match(lower(_raw),"rawconverter|parse|invalid json|reject"),1,0)
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=if(match(lower(_raw),"raw"),1,0)
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_introspection sourcetype=splunk_resource_usage earliest=-4h@h latest=now
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval hec_token="introspection_host"
      | eval hec_channel="resource"
      | eval uri_path="/introspection/splunk_resource_usage"
      | eval status=0
      | eval clientip="introspection"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(trim(toString(coalesce('data.mem_used',mem_used,0))))
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="splunk_resource_usage"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_introspection"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=0
      | eval peer_id=""
      | eval pipeline_set=""
      | eval time_in_pipeline=0.0
      | eval event_count=1
      | eval err_hit=0
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=0
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
    [ search index=_internal sourcetype=splunkd earliest=-4h@h latest=now (group=tcpin_connections OR group=mpool OR (group=pipeline AND name=indexerpipe))
      | eval cluster=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(cluster,splunk_server,host))))
      | eval host=lower(trim(toString(host)))
      | eval hec_token="tcpin_ack_lane"
      | eval hec_channel=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(group,name,"tcpin"))))
      | eval uri_path="/internal/metrics/tcpin_ack_proxy"
      | eval status=0
      | eval clientip="metrics_tcpin"
      | eval bytes=tonumber(coalesce(bytes,kilobytes,0))
      | eval duration_us=0
      | eval useragent="splunkd_metrics"
      | eval splunk_hec_instance=""
      | eval lane="hec_tcpin_ack"
      | eval is_http=0
      | eval tcpin_acks_outstanding=tonumber(coalesce(tcpin_acks_outstanding,acks_outstanding,outstanding,current_size,0))
      | eval peer_id=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(peer_id,peer,""))))
      | eval pipeline_set=lower(trim(toString(coalesce(pipeline_set,""))))
      | eval time_in_pipeline=tonumber(coalesce(time_in_pipeline,0))
      | eval event_count=tonumber(coalesce(event_count,1))
      | eval err_hit=if(coalesce(tcpin_acks_outstanding,0)>10000,1,0)
      | eval r429=0
      | eval is_raw=0
      | eval gzip_hint=0
      | eval firehose_hint=0
      | fields _time cluster host hec_token hec_channel uri_path status clientip bytes duration_us useragent splunk_hec_instance lane is_http tcpin_acks_outstanding peer_id pipeline_set time_in_pipeline event_count err_hit r429 is_raw gzip_hint firehose_hint ]
| eval cluster=coalesce(cluster,"unknown_cluster")
| eval host=coalesce(host,"unknown_host")
| eval hec_token=coalesce(hec_token,"unknown_hec_token")
| sort 0 cluster host hec_token lane - _time
| streamstats window=20 current=t global=f first(_time) AS ws0 last(_time) AS ws1 sum(eval(if(is_http==1,bytes,0))) AS http_byte_win sum(eval(if(is_http==1,err_hit,0))) AS http_err_win count(eval(if(is_http==1,1,0))) AS http_n_win BY cluster host hec_token
| eval kbps_est=if(is_http==1 AND (ws1-ws0)>0, round((http_byte_win/1024)/((ws1-ws0)+0.0001),6), null())
| eventstats median(kbps_est) AS kbps_med BY cluster hec_token
| eventstats median(eval(if(lane=="hec_tcpin_ack",tcpin_acks_outstanding,null()))) AS ack_med_host BY cluster host
| stats max(_time) AS last_seen sum(eval(if(is_http==1,err_hit,0))) AS http_err_sum count(eval(if(is_http==1,1,0))) AS http_cnt sum(eval(if(is_http==1,r429,0))) AS r429_sum sum(eval(if(is_http==1,bytes,0))) AS http_bytes_sum max(eval(if(lane=="hec_tcpin_ack",tcpin_acks_outstanding,0))) AS ack_backlog_peak max(kbps_est) AS kbps_peak max(err_hit) AS handler_err_peak dc(eval(if(is_http==1,clientip,null()))) AS clientip_richness dc(splunk_hec_instance) AS hec_instance_skew dc(hec_channel) AS channel_skew_n sum(eval(if(is_http==1,firehose_hint,0))) AS firehose_hint_sum sum(eval(if(is_http==1,gzip_hint,0))) AS gzip_hint_sum sum(eval(if(is_http==1,is_raw,0))) AS raw_post_sum values(lane) AS lanes latest(uri_path) AS uri_path_latest latest(useragent) AS useragent_latest BY cluster host hec_token
| eval token=hec_token
| eval err_rate_proxy=if(http_cnt>0, round(http_err_sum/http_cnt,4), 0)
| eval r429_rate=if(http_cnt>0, round(r429_sum/http_cnt,4), 0)
| eval coalesce_lane=mvindex(mvdedup(lanes),0)
| join type=left max=0 token
    [| inputlookup hec_token_inventory.csv
     | eval token=lower(trim(toString(token)))
     | fields token token_name owner expected_sourcetype expected_daily_volume_gb parallel_ingest_pipelines ]
| eval parallel_ingest_pipelines=tonumber(coalesce(parallel_ingest_pipelines,8))
| eval daily_vol_bytes=tonumber(coalesce(expected_daily_volume_gb,1))*1024*1024*1024
| eval quota_pressure=if(daily_vol_bytes>0 AND coalesce(http_bytes_sum,0)>0, round(http_bytes_sum/daily_vol_bytes,4), null())
| join type=left max=0 host
    [| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS perf_cpu latest(Performance.mem_used) AS perf_mem FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-4h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
     | rename Performance.host AS host ]
| eval severity=case(
    coalesce(err_rate_proxy,0)>=0.08 AND match(coalesce_lane,"hec_splunkd_access"), "page",
    coalesce(http_err_sum,0)>=40 AND match(coalesce_lane,"hec_splunkd_access"), "page",
    coalesce(ack_backlog_peak,0)>=20000, "page",
    coalesce(handler_err_peak,0)>=1 AND match(coalesce_lane,"hec_handler_log"), "page",
    coalesce(r429_rate,0)>=0.15, "page",
    coalesce(quota_pressure,0)>=0.85, "page",
    coalesce(err_rate_proxy,0)>=0.03, "warn",
    coalesce(r429_rate,0)>=0.05, "warn",
    coalesce(ack_backlog_peak,0)>=5000, "warn",
    coalesce(hec_instance_skew,0)>=4 AND coalesce(http_cnt,0)>=50, "warn",
    coalesce(channel_skew_n,0)>=8 AND coalesce(http_cnt,0)>=80, "warn",
    coalesce(clientip_richness,0)>=25 AND match(token,"token_in_header_or_query"), "warn",
    true(), "info")
| eval recommended_action=case(
    coalesce(err_rate_proxy,0)>=0.08, "triage_4xx_5xx_mix_validate_tokens_indexes_lb_routes_and_ack_urls",
    coalesce(r429_rate,0)>=0.05, "relieve_token_quota_or_shard_tokens_review_firehose_otel_batch_tuning",
    coalesce(ack_backlog_peak,0)>=5000, "inspect_indexer_ack_backlog_tcpin_metrics_rotate_peers_under_maintenance",
    coalesce(handler_err_peak,0)>=1, "review_httpinputdatahandler_rawconverter_logs_fix_malformed_payloads",
    coalesce(quota_pressure,0)>=0.55, "compare_bytes_to_hec_token_inventory_expected_daily_volume_expand_capacity",
    coalesce(hec_instance_skew,0)>=4, "rebalance_lb_sticky_sessions_or_splunk_hec_instance_affinity_behind_vip",
    true(), "refresh_hec_token_inventory_and_monitoring_console_http_event_collector_views")
| table cluster host token token_name owner coalesce_lane channel_skew_n clientip_richness hec_instance_skew err_rate_proxy r429_rate ack_backlog_peak quota_pressure perf_cpu perf_mem expected_sourcetype uri_path_latest useragent_latest gzip_hint_sum raw_post_sum firehose_hint_sum severity recommended_action last_seen
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=true latest(Performance.cpu_load_percent) AS cpu_load latest(Performance.mem_used) AS mem_used FROM datamodel=Performance WHERE nodename=Performance earliest=-24h@h latest=now BY Performance.host
| rename Performance.host AS hec_host
| table hec_host cpu_load mem_used
```

## Visualization

Severity-colored table by cluster, host, and token with drilldowns to splunkd_access rows, metrics group=hec lines, HttpInputDataHandler events, introspection JSON, tcpin proxy metrics, and inventory columns for owner and expected sourcetype.

- [Splunk Docs — Set up and use HTTP Event Collector with Splunk Web](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/SetupHECinSplunk)
- [Splunk Docs — Enable indexer acknowledgment for HTTP Event Collector](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/AboutHECIDXAck)
- [Splunk Docs — HTTP Event Collector troubleshooting](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Data/HECTroubleshoot)
- [Splunk Docs — Scale HTTP Event Collector throughput](https://docs.splunk.com/Documentation/Splunk/latest/Admin/ScaleHEC)
- [Splunk Lantern — Performance tuning the forwarding tier (HEC context)](https://lantern.splunk.com/Platform_Data_Management/Optimize_Data/Performance_tuning_the_forwarding_tier)
- [OpenTelemetry Collector Contrib — splunkhecexporter README](https://github.com/open-telemetry/opentelemetry-collector-contrib/blob/main/exporter/splunkhecexporter/README.md)
- [AWS Documentation — Kinesis Data Firehose HTTP endpoint delivery to Splunk](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/firehose/latest/dev/httpdelivery-splunk.html)
